title: Template Method
description: Define algorithm skeleton; override steps in subclasses.

cost.costNotes: Seeded via heuristics; validate against current pricing and expected workload (throughput, storage, retention, egress).

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Template Method is a pure code design pattern (Gang of Four behavioral pattern) that defines an algorithm skeleton in a base class with steps that can be overridden in subclasses. It requires no infrastructure, services, or runtime resources beyond the application itself. There are no monthly operational costs associated with this pattern - it's simply a way to organize code structure and inheritance hierarchies.
provenance.adoptionCost: Template Method is one of the simpler GoF patterns with low adoption cost. Implementation involves: (1) Creating an abstract base class with the template method containing the algorithm skeleton, (2) Defining abstract/hook methods for customizable steps, (3) Creating concrete subclasses that override specific steps. With modern AI coding assistants, developers can quickly scaffold the pattern structure. The main effort is in identifying the invariant algorithm structure versus variant steps (2-4 hours), refactoring existing code to use the pattern (4-6 hours), and basic testing (2-3 hours). Most developers understand inheritance and method overriding, so the learning curve is minimal. Total estimated effort: 8-13 hours at ~$100/hour blended rate = $800-$1300, but with AI assistance reducing this by 60-70%, resulting in approximately $300.
provenance.licenseCost: Template Method is a language-agnostic design pattern that uses standard object-oriented programming features (inheritance, abstract classes/methods) available in all major OOP languages (Java, C#, Python, TypeScript, etc.). No special tools, frameworks, or libraries are required beyond the standard language compiler/interpreter. There are no licensing fees, proprietary tools, or commercial dependencies needed to implement this pattern.

provides.reasoning: Template Method provides a skeletal algorithm structure in the base class that defines the overall flow and sequence of steps. This framework allows subclasses to extend specific steps while maintaining the core algorithmic structure.

The pattern enables subclasses to override specific steps of the algorithm through method overriding while preserving the overall algorithm flow defined in the base class. This customization mechanism is a fundamental capability of the pattern.

Template Method promotes code reuse by defining common algorithm steps once in the base class, eliminating duplication across subclasses that share the same algorithmic structure. Only variant steps need to be implemented in subclasses.

The pattern implements the Hollywood Principle ('Don't call us, we'll call you') where the base class controls the algorithm flow and calls subclass implementations at designated points. This inverts the typical control flow by having the framework call application code rather than vice versa.

requires.reasoning: Template Method fundamentally requires inheritance or subclassing mechanism to function. The pattern cannot be implemented without the ability to extend a base class that defines the algorithm skeleton.

The pattern requires the ability to override methods in subclasses, as this is the core mechanism by which algorithm steps are customized. Without method overriding, subclasses cannot provide their own implementations of algorithmic steps.

supports_nfr.reason: Template Method organises code behavior by defining algorithm structure through inheritance, not data content; it applies regardless of whether PII is present in the system.
Template Method is a behavioral code pattern; GDPR compliance obligations apply to the system, not to this pattern specifically — it is applicable regardless of GDPR requirements.
Template Method is a behavioral code pattern; CCPA compliance obligations apply to the system, not to this pattern specifically — it is applicable regardless of CCPA requirements.
Template Method is a behavioral code pattern; HIPAA compliance obligations apply to the system, not to this pattern specifically — it is applicable regardless of HIPAA requirements.
Template Method is a behavioral code pattern; SOX compliance obligations apply to the system, not to this pattern specifically — it is applicable regardless of SOX requirements.
Template Method operates below the persistence layer, defining algorithm flow through inheritance; read-your-writes consistency is enforced at the data store, not by this pattern.
Template Method does not implement persistence; strict durability guarantees are provided at the infrastructure layer and are orthogonal to this algorithm-skeleton pattern.

supports_constraints.reason: Template Method is a pure object-oriented design pattern that is cloud-agnostic.
Template Method is a fundamental Gang of Four design pattern that can be implemented in any object-oriented language.
Template Method is a code-level design pattern that defines algorithm structure independent of platform.

abstraction_level.description: Mechanism for defining the template. Abstract classes provide default implementations and state; interfaces/traits require full implementation in subclasses. Abstract classes are traditional GoF approach.

hook_methods.description: Whether to include optional hook methods for extension points. Optional hooks provide empty defaults; required hooks must be implemented; none means all steps are mandatory.

step_granularity.description: How many steps to break the algorithm into. Fine-grained offers maximum flexibility but more complexity; coarse-grained is simpler but less customizable.

invariant_enforcement.description: How to prevent subclasses from overriding the template method. Final/sealed methods enforce at compile-time; documentation relies on convention and code review.
